title: Testing the Spirits and Love Perfected
subtitle: Discerning true and false spirits, God is love, love casts out fear

heb.paragraph: Test the spirits to see whether they are from God - discernment is required because many false prophets have gone out.
Every spirit that confesses Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is from God - the christological test is incarnation.
You have overcome them, for he who is in you is greater - victory is assured because the indwelling God exceeds all opponents.

panels.ctx: Beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits to see whether they are from God, for many false prophets have gone out into the world. By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is from God, and every spirit that does not confess Jesus is not from God. This is the spirit of the antichrist, which you heard was coming and now is in the world already. Little children, you are from God and have overcome them, for he who is in you is greater than he who is in the world. They are from the world; therefore they speak from the world, and the world listens to them. We are from God. Whoever knows God listens to us; whoever is not from God does not listen to us. By this we know the Spirit of truth and the spirit of error.

cross.note: No one speaking in the Spirit of God says Jesus is accursed.
Those who do not confess Jesus Christ coming in the flesh - this is the deceiver.

notes.note: Test the spirits - many false prophets have gone out. The Spirit of God confesses Jesus Christ has come in flesh. The spirit of antichrist denies this.
You have overcome them - he who is in you is greater. They are from the world; we are from God. By this we know truth and error.
Calvin emphasizes the christological criterion: true faith confesses incarnation. Denial of Jesus humanity is the antichrist spirit.
Victory is not by our strength but by indwelling God. The greater one within guarantees triumph over external opponents.
Come in the flesh (en sarki elelythota) - perfect participle emphasizes the ongoing reality of incarnation. Jesus remains human.
Spirit of truth and spirit of error - the contrast anticipates the explicit mention of the Spirit of truth in John 14-16.
Yarbrough emphasizes that testing spirits is not optional but commanded. Discernment is a spiritual discipline.
Kruse notes that the false teachers likely denied Jesus humanity (docetism). The incarnation is non-negotiable.

heb.paragraph: God is love - not merely that God loves but that love defines his essential nature. Love is who God is.

heb.gloss: only, unique, one of a kind
heb.paragraph: God sent his only Son into the world - the unique Son was given, not created. The gift demonstrates love.
No one has ever seen God - yet if we love one another, God abides in us. Love makes the invisible God visible.

panels.ctx: Beloved, let us love one another, for love is from God, and whoever loves has been born of God and knows God. Whoever does not love does not know God, because God is love. In this the love of God was made manifest among us, that God sent his only Son into the world so that we might live through him. In this is love, not that we have loved God but that he loved us and sent his Son to be the propitiation for our sins. Beloved, if God so loved us, we also ought to love one another. No one has ever seen God; if we love one another, God abides in us and his love is perfected in us.

cross.note: God so loved the world that he gave his only Son.
God shows his love for us in that while we were sinners, Christ died for us.
No one has ever seen God; the only God who is at the Father side has made him known.
By this all people will know you are my disciples, if you have love for one another.

notes.note: Love one another, for love is from God. Whoever loves is born of God and knows God. Whoever does not love does not know God, because God is love.
God love was made manifest: he sent his only Son that we might live. This is love - not that we loved God but that he loved us and sent his Son as propitiation.
If God so loved us, we ought to love one another. No one has seen God; if we love, God abides in us and his love is perfected in us.
Calvin notes that God is love is not reversible - love is not God. Love defines God; God is the source of all true love.
God initiative is the crucial point. Love is not our achievement but our response. God loved first; we love because he loved.
God is love (ho theos agape estin) - one of the most profound theological statements in Scripture. Love is not one attribute among many but the essence.
Love is perfected (teteleiomene) - brought to completion, reaches its goal. Divine love achieves its purpose when we love.
Yarbrough emphasizes that God is love grounds the ethical imperative. Because of who God is, we must love.
Kruse notes that the invisible God becomes visible through Christian love. The church displays God character to the world.

heb.gloss: to perfect, complete, bring to maturity
heb.paragraph: By this is love perfected with us - love reaches its intended goal. Perfected love produces confidence.
There is no fear in love, but perfect love casts out fear - fear and mature love are incompatible. Fear involves punishment.
If anyone says I love God and hates his brother, he is a liar - love for visible brother tests love for invisible God.

panels.ctx: By this we know that we abide in him and he in us, because he has given us of his Spirit. And we have seen and testify that the Father has sent his Son to be the Savior of the world. Whoever confesses that Jesus is the Son of God, God abides in him and he in God. So we have come to know and to believe the love that God has for us. God is love, and whoever abides in love abides in God, and God abides in him. By this is love perfected with us, so that we may have confidence for the day of judgment, because as he is so also are we in this world. There is no fear in love, but perfect love casts out fear. For fear has to do with punishment, and whoever fears has not been perfected in love. We love because he first loved us. If anyone says I love God and hates his brother, he is a liar; for he who does not love his brother whom he has seen cannot love God whom he has not seen. And this commandment we have from him: whoever loves God must also love his brother.

cross.note: You did not receive the spirit of slavery to fall back into fear.
Deliver all those who through fear of death were subject to lifelong slavery.
We have heard for ourselves, and we know that this is indeed the Savior of the world.
You shall love the LORD your God with all your heart.

notes.note: We abide in him because he has given us his Spirit. The Father sent his Son to be Savior. Whoever confesses Jesus is the Son, God abides in him. God is love.
Love is perfected so we may have confidence for judgment day. Perfect love casts out fear. Fear has to do with punishment.
We love because he first loved us. Claiming to love God while hating brother is lying. The commandment: whoever loves God must love his brother.
Calvin notes that fear of judgment reveals incomplete love. As love matures, fear recedes. Perfect love produces bold assurance.
The logic is irrefutable: if you cannot love the visible brother, you cannot love the invisible God. Love for God is tested by love for people.
As he is so also are we in this world - believers share Christ nature and mission now, not just in the future.
Fear has to do with punishment (kolasin echei) - the fear in view is terror of condemnation, not healthy reverence.
Yarbrough emphasizes the practical test: love for brother is visible evidence of love for God. Vertical and horizontal are inseparable.
Kruse notes that the chapter climaxes with the commandment: whoever loves God must also love his brother. This is non-negotiable.

themes.note: Mercy dominates through the God is love declaration. Faith is tested by christological confession. Mission appears in the Savior of the world title.

rows.label: vv. 7-12
rows.text: God is love; sent his Son

rows.label: vv. 13-21
rows.text: Perfect love casts out fear; love brother

lit.note: Chapter 4 combines christological confession with brotherly love. God is love becomes the ground of ethics and the source of confidence.
